transport.airport_to_city: 15 min by train from Schiphol to Centraal
transport.city_card: I Amsterdam City Card — includes all museums + unlimited transport
transport.tips: A bike is often the fastest way to get around. OV-chipkaart is the reloadable transport card used across all modes.

neighbourhoods.Jordaan: Charming canals, independent shops, cosy brown cafes. Best for romantic stays.
neighbourhoods.De Pijp: Young and diverse, great for food and nightlife. Home to the Albert Cuyp market.
neighbourhoods.Museum Quarter: Near the big three museums. Quiet residential feel.
neighbourhoods.Centrum/Canal Ring: UNESCO canal belt. Most central but touristy.
neighbourhoods.Plantage: East Amsterdam, zoo, botanical garden. Quiet and leafy.
neighbourhoods.Noord: Post-industrial creative district. Trendy but off the beaten path.

practical_info.tipping: Not mandatory but 5-10% appreciated for good service
practical_info.safety: Very safe city. Watch for bikes — they always have right of way.
practical_info.tap_water: Tap water is clean and safe to drink
practical_info.common_scam: Keep valuables secure on busy trams and at Centraal station

highlights: Van Gogh Museum — world's largest Van Gogh collection
Anne Frank House — moving WWII history
Albert Cuyp market on Saturday mornings

transport.airport_to_city: RER B to Gare du Nord ~35 min from CDG
transport.city_card: Paris Visite pass — unlimited metro/bus/RER + museum discounts
transport.tips: The Paris Métro is fast and comprehensive. Carnets (books of 10 tickets) offer better value than single fares.

neighbourhoods.Le Marais: Historic Jewish quarter, now LGBTQ+ hub and fashionable district. Museums, galleries, restaurants.
neighbourhoods.Saint-Germain: Classic Left Bank intellectual district. Cafés, bookshops, galleries.
neighbourhoods.Montmartre: Bohemian hilltop village. Artists, Sacré-Cœur, vineyard, and the best city views.
neighbourhoods.Bastille: Lively neighbourhood, good for nightlife, jazz clubs, and authentic brasseries.
neighbourhoods.Champs-Élysées: Tourist and luxury shopping boulevard. Expensive but iconic.
neighbourhoods.Latin Quarter: Student neighbourhood around the Sorbonne. Cheap eats, bookshops, museums.

practical_info.tipping: Service charge included by law. Small tips (€1-2) appreciated.
practical_info.language_tip: Always try 'Bonjour' first — Parisians respond much better to any attempt at French.
practical_info.safety: Generally safe. Be alert in crowded tourist areas and on the Métro.
practical_info.tap_water: Wallace fountains throughout the city offer free, clean drinking water
practical_info.common_scam: Gold ring scam at tourist sites, petition clipboards near Sacré-Cœur

highlights: Musée du Louvre — Mona Lisa and so much more
Marais food walk and Le Falafel

transport.airport_to_city: Aerobus to Plaça Catalunya ~35 min / Metro L9 ~40 min
transport.city_card: Barcelona Card — metro + bus unlimited + museum discounts
transport.tips: T-Casual (10-trip metro card) is the best value. The city is very walkable between Gothic Quarter and Eixample.

neighbourhoods.Gothic Quarter: Ancient heart of the city. Dense medieval streets, best for walking.
neighbourhoods.El Born: Fashionable boutiques, tapas bars, Picasso Museum. Very lively evenings.
neighbourhoods.Eixample: Modernista architecture, Gaudí buildings, upscale shops and restaurants.
neighbourhoods.Barceloneta: Beach neighbourhood. Seafood restaurants and summer beach culture.
neighbourhoods.Gràcia: Bohemian, local neighbourhood with lively plazas and independent restaurants.
neighbourhoods.Poblenou: Former industrial district, now a tech and creative hub with beach access.

practical_info.tipping: Not obligatory. 5-10% for good restaurant service is appreciated.
practical_info.language_tip: Catalan is the official language. Spanish widely spoken. A few words of Catalan are warmly received.
practical_info.safety: Watch for pickpockets on Las Ramblas and the Barceloneta beach area.
practical_info.tap_water: Safe to drink but many locals prefer bottled water for the taste
practical_info.common_scam: The shell game and card tricks on Las Ramblas. Avoid participating.

highlights: Park Güell — mosaic terraces and city views
Picasso Museum in the Gothic Quarter
La Boqueria market at breakfast time

transport.airport_to_city: Leonardo Express train to Roma Termini ~30 min from FCO
transport.city_card: Roma Pass — public transport + free museums entry
transport.tips: Rome is compact and best explored on foot. The 2-line Metro is limited — buses and trams fill the gaps. Validate your ticket every time.

neighbourhoods.Trastevere: The most romantic neighbourhood. Cobblestone lanes, trattorias, and vibrant evening atmosphere.
neighbourhoods.Campo de' Fiori: Lively market by day, buzzing social scene by night. Central and atmospheric.
neighbourhoods.Prati: Upscale neighbourhood near the Vatican. Quiet streets, excellent restaurants, local feel.
neighbourhoods.Testaccio: Rome's food neighbourhood. The old slaughterhouse market, authentic trattorias.
neighbourhoods.Monti: Bohemian, vintage shops, wine bars. The trendiest neighbourhood for young locals.
neighbourhoods.Centro Storico: Historic centre with the Pantheon, Piazza Navona, and the Jewish Ghetto.

practical_info.tipping: Cover charge (coperto) is standard. A few euros for good service is appreciated.
practical_info.language_tip: Italian is essential for non-touristy restaurants — staff will warm to you immediately.
practical_info.safety: Pickpockets on the Metro (especially Line A) and at the Colosseum. Be vigilant.
practical_info.tap_water: Rome's nasoni (street fountains) flow with clean, cold water 24/7. Always free.
practical_info.common_scam: Unsolicited rose giving near tourist sites, extra charges added to restaurant bills

highlights: Colosseum and Roman Forum — dawn entry recommended
Pompeii day trip from Roma Termini

transport.airport_to_city: Metro Red Line to Oriente or Alameda ~20 min
transport.city_card: Lisboa Card — all public transport + free museums entry
transport.tips: Lisbon is hilly — the historic trams are slow but charming. The Metro is fast for longer distances. NAVT card for unlimited transport.

neighbourhoods.Alfama: The ancient Moorish quarter. Fado music, viewpoints, São Jorge Castle. Most romantic.
neighbourhoods.Bairro Alto: Bohemian nightlife district. Packed with fado houses, bars, and restaurants.
neighbourhoods.Chiado: Elegant shopping and café district. Fernando Pessoa's favourite café, A Brasileira.
neighbourhoods.Belém: Riverside neighbourhood. Jerónimos Monastery, Belém Tower, pastel de nata.
neighbourhoods.LX Factory: Post-industrial creative hub. Sunday market, restaurants, design shops.
neighbourhoods.Príncipe Real: Lisbon's most refined neighbourhood. Antique shops, garden squares, luxury hotels.

practical_info.tipping: Not obligatory. Round up or leave 5-10% for restaurant service.
practical_info.language_tip: Portuguese is different from Spanish — locals greatly appreciate any attempt at it.
practical_info.safety: One of Europe's safest capitals. Standard city precautions apply.
practical_info.tap_water: Safe to drink throughout the city
practical_info.common_scam: Overpriced tourist restaurants near Alfama viewpoints — walk one street back for better value

highlights: Alfama fado show — book a casa de fado in advance
Pastel de nata at Pastéis de Belém
